core.title: regulatory agency
core.description: public authority or government agency responsible for exercising authority over something in a regulatory or supervisory capacity

core.title: primary federal regulator
core.description: federal regulatory agency that is designated as the main agency responsible for oversight of a given institution for an institution

core.title: self-regulating organization
core.description: non-governmental organization that has the power to create and exercise some degree of regulatory authority over an industry or profession in some country or group of countries

core.title: fund supervisory authority
core.description: party that supervises the fund or fund industry

core.title: government agency
core.description: permanent or semi-permanent organization, often an appointed commission, in the machinery of government that is responsible for the oversight and administration of specific functions

core.title: government body
core.description: formal organization that is an agency, instrumentality, or other body of a supranational, national, federal, state, or local government, including certain multijurisdictional agencies and departments that carry out the business of government

core.title: business registration authority
core.description: registration authority that is responsible for maintaining a registry of business entities

core.title: regulated exchange
core.description: regulated market that is operated by and/or managed by a market operator that brings together or facilitates the bringing together of multiple third-party buying and selling interests in financial instruments

core.title: regulatory capacity
core.description: the capacity of some natural person to regulate some industry, organization, or product by virtue of some certification program on behalf of some regulatory agency

core.title: regulatory service
core.description: service provided by a regulatory agency, which may include, but not be limited to, examination, monitoring, supervision, testing, or other capabilities required to ensure the integrity, fairness, safety, or other capacity of a given industry, organization, or product

core.title: regulation
core.description: a rule used to carry out a law

core.title: regulation identifier
core.description: an identifier associated with a regulation

core.title: regulation identification scheme
core.description: a scheme for organizing information and allocating identifiers to regulations

core.title: reg b
core.description: US regulation concerning "Equal credit opportunity act" Electronic delivery of disclosures

core.title: reg z
core.description: US Fed regulation "Truth in Lending Act" uniform standards for electronic delivery of disclosures

core.title: legal obligation
core.description: an obligation or duty that is enforceable by a court

core.title: report
core.description: document that provides a structured description of something, prepared on ad hoc, periodic, recurring, regular, or as required basis

core.title: regulatory report
core.description: document required to support operational transparency that demonstrates compliance with some specification, law, policy, restriction, or other rule specified by a regulatory agency

core.title: rating report
core.description: report describing a set of ratings

core.title: credit report
core.description: report describing the creditworthiness and related credit attributes of a borrower

core.title: credit report product
core.description: branded credit report offered in the marketplace

core.title: remittance report
core.description: Report containing a specific and limited set of information about the Deal. Term origin:MBS PoC Reviews

core.title: HMDA report
core.description: a report prepared to satisfy HMDA regulatory reporting requirements as described US section 1003.3(c) of the Revised Home Mortgage Disclosure Act of 2015

core.title: income verification report
core.description: report providing the methodology and supporting documentation used to verify the borrower's income

core.title: disclosure provision
core.description: contractual provision that outlines the requirements and responsibilities of one or both parties to reveal certain information to each other

core.title: disclosure requirement
core.description: Requirement for disclosure to borrowers or potential borrowers. There are two kinds of disclosure requirement: 1. Disclosure about the information held on the borrower 2. Disclsure about the product.

core.title: product disclosure requirement
core.description: A requirement governing what representations can be made about a product, as it affects the consumer.

core.title: loan regulatory requirement
core.description: A regulatory requirement defined in regulations by a comsumer credit act or other legislation.

core.title: consumer credit requirement
core.description: Requirement set out on the lender about how they must treat the appliction to a loan

core.title: data protection requirement
core.description: Requirements defining how data about individuals is held. Example is the EU DA directive and laws, which make the data the property of the individual that data is about. Covers - what information i sheld - who information can be divulged to. - the individual's rights in respect of that information Privacy regulations cover most of this. EU defines "Personal Data" and "Sensitive Personal Data". For credit reference agencies the latter would be covered. More detail about whether they can divulge facts which are not subject to formal judgements etc.

core.title: reporting party
core.description: party providing a report, typically in response to some contractual, legal, regulatory or other business requirement

core.title: trade reporting facility
core.description: facility that provides a mechanism for the reporting of transactions effected otherwise than on an exchange

core.title: approved reporting mechanism
core.description: data reporting services provider that is authorized to provide the service of reporting details of transactions to competent authorities or ESMA (the European Securities and Markets Authority) on behalf of investment firms

core.title: data reporting services provider
core.description: market data provider and reporting party that reports and/or publishes data on securities transactions, including required regulatory reporting for such transactions, and as such is subject to regulatory supervision

core.title: business register identifier
core.description: identifier that uniquely identifies a business register, such as a register identified by the Global Legal Entity Identifier Foundation (GLEIF) registration authorities list

core.title: legal entity identifier
core.description: an organization identifier that uniquely identifies a legal person as defined in ISO 17442

core.title: legal entity identifier registry
core.description: registry for registering and maintaining information about business entities for a particular jurisdiction

core.title: universal loan identifier
core.description: unique identifier given to unequivocally identify a specific loan secured by real estate

core.title: Nationwide Mortgage Licensing System and Registry Identifier
core.description: the number permanently assigned by the Nationwide Mortgage Licensing System and Registry (NMLS) for each company, branch, and individual that maintains a single account on NMLS.

core.title: regulatory action
core.description: action initiated by a regulatory agency that affects a legal entity or the securities it issues and may have a material impact on that entity's stakeholders, such as shareholders and creditors

core.title: disclosure action
core.description: corporate action involving a request for holders or beneficial owners to disclose their name, location and holdings of any issue to the issuer

core.title: credit rating agency
core.description: rating agency that publishes reports assessing the creditworthiness of a borrower or legal entity, either generally or with respect to a specific obligation

core.title: rating agency
core.description: rating issuer that is also a rating scale publisher, frequently but not always an independent rating service

core.title: HMDA covered loan contract
core.description: a closed-end mortgage loan or open-end line of credit that is not an excluded transaction for HMDA reporting under US section 1003.3(c) of the Revised Home Mortgage Disclosure Act of 2015
